    AI Overview non è disponibile per questa ricercaImpossibile generare un'overview dell'AI in questo momento. Riprova più tardi.
    AI Overview
    Climate change is driven by human activities that amplify the natural "greenhouse effect". By burning fossil fuels, cutting down forests, and engaging in intensive agriculture, humanity has released unprecedented amounts of heat-trapping gases into the atmosphere, causing global temperatures to rise. 
    The primary drivers of this warming fall into the following core categories: 
    • Burning Fossil Fuels (68% of emissions): Coal, oil, and natural gas used for electricity generation, transportation, and industrial manufacturing are the largest contributors to global warming, releasing massive amounts of carbon dioxide (
      CO2cap C cap O sub 2
      ).
    • Deforestation: Forests act as natural carbon sinks, absorbing
      CO2cap C cap O sub 2
      . When trees are cut down or burned, this stored carbon is released back into the atmosphere, diminishing the Earth's capacity to regulate greenhouse levels.
    • Agriculture and Livestock: Intensive farming practices emit significant quantities of powerful greenhouse gases. Methane (
      CH4cap C cap H sub 4
      ) is produced by livestock during digestion and from manure, while nitrous oxide (
      N2Ocap N sub 2 cap O
      ) is heavily released through the use of synthetic fertilizers.
    • Industrial Processes: Manufacturing sectors (such as cement and steel production) require immense amounts of energy and rely on chemical reactions that directly emit
      CO2cap C cap O sub 2
       and highly potent fluorinated gases.       
    Learn more about the science behind these drivers and how they are impacting the planet in the United Nations Causes and Effects Overview or review the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) reports on the NASA Climate Change Science platform.

    What is the main cause of climate change essay?
    Human activities such as deforestation, burning fossil fuels, farming livestock etc., generate an enormous amount of greenhouse gases. This results in the greenhouse effect and global warming which are the major causes of climate change.

    500+ Words Essay on Climate Change - BYJU'S


    BYJU'S
    https://byjus.com › cbse › climate-change-essay
    BYJU'S
    https://byjus.com › cbse › climate-change-essay
    What are the big 5 natural causes of climate change?
    These have been caused by many natural factors, including changes in the sun, emissions from volcanoes, variations in Earth's orbit and levels of carbon dioxide (CO2). Global climate change has typically occurred very slowly, over thousands or millions of years.

    What causes the Earth's climate to change? - British Geological Survey

    https://www.bgs.ac.uk › discovering-geology › what-cau...
    British Geological Survey
    https://www.bgs.ac.uk › discovering-geology › what-cau...
    What are the three worst causes of climate change?
    Burning fossil fuels, cutting down forests and farming livestock are increasingly influencing the climate and the earth's temperature. This adds enormous amounts of greenhouse gases to those naturally occurring in the atmosphere, increasing the greenhouse effect and global warming.
